source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design one representative 15 m clear-span simple-supported glulam-timber pedestrian footbridge compliant with the EWB-UK Technical Handbook (2022) and aligned to Eurocode 5 (EN 1995-1-1) and Eurocode 1 load codes for a low-resource rural setting. The bridge is an explicit multi-part assembly. It consists of (A) two parallel GL28h glulam timber main beams h = 600 mm b = 200 mm, 15 m long, simply supported on concrete abutments; (B) transverse cross-timber deck planks 50 mm thick forming the 1.2 m clear walkway, typically 150 mm wide on 400 mm centers along the 15 m span; (C) timber handrail framing on both sides (posts, top/mid rails, balusters) 1100 mm tall; (D) steel angle brackets and screw/bolt fasteners connecting deck planks and handrail posts to the main glulam beams and connecting the beam ends into abutment shoes; (E) elastomeric / steel shoe bearings at each beam end seated on the concrete abutments. Design pedestrian live load is 5.0 kN/m2 uniformly distributed per EN 1991-2 (footbridges), design wind pressure is 1.0 kN/m2 per simplified EN 1991-1-4 (exposure corresponding to rural inland, force coefficient 1.3 on solid envelope), plus self-weight of all parts. Design safety factors on material strength per Eurocode 5: gamma_M,glulam = 1.25, k_mod for medium-term load = 0.8, so design bending strength f_m,d = k_mod * f_m,k / gamma_M = 0.8 * 28.0 / 1.25 = 17.9 MPa for GL28h. Deflection limit L/300 = 15000/300 = 50 mm under live load (EN 1995-1-1 serviceability). The bridge includes a 1100 mm tall timber handrail on both sides (EN 1991-1-7 horizontal line load 1.0 kN/m on top rail). Deliverable is a multi-body CAD assembly of the glulam beams, deck planks, handrails, bracket / fastener set, and abutment bearings, suitable for linear static structural FEA per three Eurocode-aligned load combinations: LC1 pedestrian UDL + dead, LC2 wind + dead, LC3 pedestrian + wind + dead (accompanying combination). Interfaces: beam-to-deck is screwed or bolted through steel angle at each plank crossing; handrail post-to-deck-or-beam is bolted through angle bracket; beam end-to-abutment is seated on an elastomeric or steel bearing shoe. Vibration (EN 1995-2 Annex B), thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling analyses are NOT required in this baseline sample but lateral-torsional buckling of glulam beams is envelope-checked by Eurocode hand calc. Deliver STEP AP242 multi-body with one body per glulam beam, one body per plank, one body per handrail component, one body per bracket/fastener instance, and one body per bearing, each tagged with part_family and instance_index; report per-part max stress and assembly deflection. FEM pass/fail is set on the assembled model.
short_prompt:
Design a 15 m glulam pedestrian footbridge as a multi-part timber-steel assembly per EWB-UK Technical Handbook (2022) and Eurocode 5 EN 1995-1-1.

Main beams GL28h 600 x 200 mm; transverse crossbeams and deck planks treated softwood; partial factors gamma_M = 1.25 and modification factor k_mod = 0.8 giving design bending resistance f_m,d = 17.9 MPa.

Load cases: LC1 pedestrian live 5.0 kN/m2 uniformly distributed per EN 1991-2; LC2 wind 1.0 kN/m2 lateral per EN 1991-1-4; LC3 handrail line load 1.0 kN/m; LC4 self-weight.

Pass/fail: deflection under live <= L/300 = 50 mm; timber member utilisation <= 1.0 with k_mod and gamma_M; connection DCR <= 1.0 at assembled level.

prompt.title: 15 m EWB-UK glulam footbridge to Eurocode 5 (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part 15 m glulam footbridge: two GL28h 600 x 200 mm glulam main beams, transverse 50 mm timber deck planks forming a 1.2 m walkway, timber handrails on both sides, steel angle brackets + screws at every interface, and bearing shoes on concrete abutments. Carries 5 kPa pedestrian + 1 kPa wind + rail line load with Eurocode 5 partial factors. FEM pass/fail is set on the assembled model.

parts.name: glulam_main_beam
parts.role: longitudinal GL28h glulam main beam carrying live + wind + dead over the 15 m clear span

geometric_constraints.support_condition: simply supported on abutments via bearing shoe

parts.material_hint: GL28h glulam, f_m,k = 28.0 MPa, gamma_M = 1.25, k_mod = 0.8

parts.name: timber_deck_plank
parts.role: transverse deck plank forming the walking surface across the 1.2 m walkway

parts.name: handrail_component
parts.role: timber handrail framing (post, top rail, mid rail, baluster) on both sides of the walkway; 1100 mm tall
parts.material_hint: C24 timber or light steel, team-selected

parts.name: steel_bracket_and_fastener
parts.role: galvanized steel angle + screw/bolt connecting deck planks, handrail posts, and beam ends to the glulam main beams / abutments

geometric_constraints.fastener_size: lag screw 8-10 mm or bolt 10-12 mm
geometric_constraints.count_note: approximately one or two brackets per plank crossing, one per handrail post, one per beam end

parts.material_hint: hot-dip galvanized structural steel; stainless fasteners in wet exposure

parts.name: beam_bearing_shoe
parts.role: elastomeric or steel bearing shoe at each glulam beam end seated on the concrete abutment
parts.material_hint: neoprene elastomeric pad or galvanized steel shoe

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: x = span (15 m), y = transverse (1.2 m walkway + handrails), z = vertical

boundary_conditions.supports: pinned + roller simple supports at abutments (via bearing shoes)
boundary_conditions.beam_to_deck: screwed/bolted through D-family brackets at each plank crossing; model as coupled pair
boundary_conditions.handrail_to_deck_or_beam: bolted through angle at post base; model as coupled
boundary_conditions.beam_end_to_abutment: seated on E-family bearing shoe; pinned at one end, roller at the other

objective.primary: pass Eurocode 5 ULS + SLS on the assembled model

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: multipart acceptance is set on the assembled FEA; per-part stress is reported but not gated

interfaces.mediated_by: bonded/seated (bearing shoe under glulam beam end)
interfaces.load_path: vertical reaction + longitudinal thermal expansion into abutment
pedestrian UDL from planks into glulam main beams
handrail horizontal line load + wind on railing into deck / rim

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted (D-family angle to beam side face)
interfaces.load_path: alternate handrail-post anchorage path into glulam where post aligns with beam

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A 15 m glulam + timber pedestrian footbridge is fabricated as several glulam beams, dozens of cross planks, handrail framing on both sides, dozens of steel bracket-fastener pairs, and discrete bearing shoes at each abutment; a monolithic idealization loses the load path at every interface.
notes.speculation_caveats: Counts for deck planks (400 mm on-center over 15 m), handrail components (2 m post spacing), and bracket/fastener instances are inferred; the original sample names typical spacings but not individual part counts.
